channel.title: Education on Indiana Legislative Tools
channel.description: Recent content in Education on Indiana Legislative Tools

item.title: Education
item.description:
Education is a cornerstone issue in Indiana's legislative agenda. The state has been working to improve its education system through various initiatives, including increasing funding for K-12 schools, implementing new programs to help students succeed, and addressing teacher compensation.
Recent focus areas include literacy, workforce development programs, school choice options, and enhanced teacher recruitment and retention strategies.

item.title: School Funding Reform
item.description:
Bill Summary

HB 1002 would reform the school funding formula to provide more equitable distribution of resources to schools across Indiana, with a focus on addressing disparities between urban and rural districts.

Key Provisions

  • Increases per-pupil funding by 5% for all schools
  • Creates a new “rural support” index in the funding formula
  • Establishes grants for schools with high populations of students in poverty
  • Requires annual reporting on funding equity metrics

Current Status

The bill was introduced on January 10, 2025, and has been referred to the House Education Committee. A hearing is scheduled for January 25, 2025.
